why: The dashed 8-4-4-4-12 UUID form is REJECTED (validate.ts). A path param carrying the human UUID form must 400, never be re-derived.
Case-sensitivity: the grammar is lowercase-hex only. An uppercase digit must be rejected so two casings of one id cannot address two rows.
Length is fixed at 32. A short value must be rejected before any body parse or crypto (the M2 fail-fast gate).
The '.>' tail is REQUIRED — the scope is a subtree grant. A prefix without the wildcard is a different (narrower) subject and must not be silently widened.

MEMBERSHIP-BOUNDARY reject (D15/§12/§14). The membership half derives requested_scope from the same principal ids the (carved-out) rate-limit half keys on. Here, at the membership boundary, an out-of-grammar principal ('_' is absent from principal-id's kebab-strict alphabet, RFC-0001) MUST be ENFORCED-not-coerced — rejected, never mapped to a fallback. The charset-coercion KV-KEY collision family (admissionKeyPrincipalSegment) is a rate-limit-plane concern and CARVED to RFC-0010; RFC-0006 keeps only this requested_scope reject.
Case-sensitivity: the enum is UPPERCASE. A case-insensitive parser would accept 'admitted' and silently confer membership — the grammar pins case so ABNF cannot mask it.
GRANTED is the pre-ADR-0015 issuance vocabulary; it MUST NOT be honoured on the admission enum. An unknown token is a fault, never coerced to the nearest state.

ENFORCING vector for D6/§7.3 (not a why-string). claim.peer_pubkey (...MDE=) != row.peer_pubkey (...MDI=) MUST be rejected 409 identity_mismatch even though the request_id and network match — the admin signature must commit to the exact key admitted. Threat model puts a dishonest/buggy registry in scope; a registry can violate any immutability rule it alone enforces, so identity coherence rides the SIGNED message.

ENFORCING vector for D6/§7.3 — the CROSS-NETWORK confused-deputy. Binding peer_pubkey ALONE does not catch this: the SAME peer_pubkey legitimately holds distinct PENDING rows across networks (the (principal_id, peer_pubkey, network_id) triple), so a request_id -> wrong-network-row substitution still matches the key. claim.network_id (metafactory) != row.network_id (othernet) MUST be rejected 409 identity_mismatch. Only binding network_id closes it; this is what makes the §7.4 per-network authorization rule cryptographically load-bearing.

ENFORCING vector for D8/§8.3. claim.peer_pubkey (...MDE=) != the addressed entry's target_stack_pubkey (...MDI=) MUST be rejected 409 identity_mismatch — catching a hub-admin fumbling the target row/entry at write time and giving the member a signed intended-recipient assertion. Under a per-principal admission the SEAL is a per-stack write (§8.1); this binding is what makes the per-key sealed_secrets[] delivery safe (a shared/mis-targeted seal would collapse per-stack subject isolation, which the v2 leaf_user guard exists to reject).

§8.1 / §14 R7 fetch-seam ENFORCING vector (D9). The DECODE-then-COMPARE guard: a v2 credential minted for a DIFFERENT subject (leaf_user 'mallory/meta-factory') than the member's own identities ('andreas/meta-factory') MUST be refused, not installed — else a courier sealing another member's real creds to this member would land. §14 today has only a decode-seam field-presence vector; this binds the R7 install-time comparison the reference impl deploys fail-closed (cortex fetch-sealed-secret.ts:118-139, PR #1609 / C-1597 closed). The leaf_user label is named in the reason; the creds text is NEVER echoed.

§8.1 / §14 R7 FAIL-CLOSED limb (D9). A v2 credential with NOTHING to bind it to — the caller supplied no expected identities — MUST be refused, never defaulted-open, even though leaf_user is itself well-formed. Binds the reference impl's fail-closed branch (cortex fetch-sealed-secret.ts:124-131, expected.length === 0 -> refuse). A v2 credential is refused unless the caller can prove the subject is theirs.

§4.2 terminal-per-key (D3). REJECTED/REVOKED/DEPARTED are terminal PER KEY: there is NO terminal -> PENDING (or -> ADMITTED) wire path. An admit applies only to a PENDING row; on an already-decided (REVOKED) row it MUST return 409 already_decided (cortex store.ts AlreadyDecidedError). Any 'admin reset' of a terminal row is out-of-band DB surgery, not a transition this contract defines — a conforming impl MUST NOT expose a route that performs one. Re-admission after a terminal decision is KEY ROTATION, not a state reset (see the companion vector).

A KNOWN-newer version fails closed with a distinct error naming the real remedy (upgrade cortex), NOT the misleading missing-leaf_psk path. A decoder MUST NOT guess a higher-versioned payload (sealed-leaf-secret.ts UnsupportedEnvelopeVersionError).
Fails closed on a malformed v1 envelope — else the member renders a leaf with a junk secret. The decoder never echoes the plaintext in the error.
A string v:"2" must be rejected, not silently mis-routed to the v1 missing-leaf_psk path — the numeric-version guard covers every decoder (parseEnvelopeObject).
